title: Low dissolved oxygen response
trigger_cues: low DO; DO below target; oxygen limitation; poor aeration
core_statement: If dissolved oxygen is persistently low, check blower output, air distribution, diffuser condition, and basin mixing before concluding that biology is the primary cause of performance change.
caution: Do not infer filamentous bulking or nitrification failure from DO alone; verify with trend data and field inspection.
source: Placeholder engineering card; replace with plant SOP or validated reference

title: High SVI interpretation
trigger_cues: high SVI; poor settling; sludge blanket rise; bulking risk
core_statement: Elevated SVI suggests reduced settleability and should trigger review of settling behavior, sludge age control, return sludge operation, and microscopy observations.
caution: A single SVI value is not sufficient to diagnose the mechanism; confirm with repeated measurements and sludge blanket observations.
source: Placeholder engineering card; replace with plant SOP or validated reference

title: Effluent COD increase check
trigger_cues: COD_out increase; treatment deterioration; effluent worsening
core_statement: When effluent COD rises, compare influent load, aeration condition, sludge concentration, settling indicators, and recent operational changes before proposing corrective action.
caution: Do not state a root cause unless it is directly supported by available operating records and evidence.
source: Placeholder engineering card; replace with plant SOP or validated reference

title: Moderate temperature shift
trigger_cues: temperature decrease; seasonal shift; colder mixed liquor
core_statement: A moderate temperature decrease may slow biological reaction rates and should be interpreted together with sludge inventory, aeration, and effluent trends.
caution: Avoid claiming a quantitative kinetic impact unless temperature-response evidence is available for the specific plant.
source: Placeholder engineering card; replace with plant SOP or validated reference

title: Microscopy use in diagnosis
trigger_cues: indicator taxa; microscopy observation; protozoa; filament
core_statement: Microscopy observations can support interpretation of sludge condition, but they should be used together with process data rather than as a stand-alone diagnosis.
caution: Do not claim species-level ecological meaning beyond what the observation quality supports.
source: Placeholder engineering card; replace with plant microscopy guide

title: Rotifer presence note
trigger_cues: rotifer observed; mature sludge; stable sludge age
core_statement: Rotifer presence may be consistent with relatively mature sludge under some operating conditions and can be noted as supporting context only.
caution: Do not present rotifers as proof of stable treatment performance; cross-check with settling and effluent data.
source: Placeholder engineering card; replace with microscopy handbook

title: Free-swimming ciliates note
trigger_cues: free-swimming ciliates; dispersed growth concern; early warning
core_statement: A visible increase in free-swimming ciliates may justify closer review of loading variation, floc structure, and settling indicators.
caution: Do not infer upset severity from microscopy alone; use it as a trigger for follow-up checks.
source: Placeholder engineering card; replace with microscopy handbook

title: SV30 low-to-moderate context
trigger_cues: SV30 trend; settling volume; sludge compaction
core_statement: SV30 should be interpreted together with MLSS and SVI because the same SV30 can imply different settling conditions at different sludge concentrations.
caution: Avoid using SV30 alone to describe settleability quality.
source: Placeholder engineering card; replace with plant SOP or validated reference
